name: Ace of Batons
interpretation: To start out the suit, a tramp clown ties his bindle to his stick as he gets ready to travel.
image_description: A tramp clown ties a bundle to a baton. The baton, a long and gnarled green stick, is in the center of the card. The clown is off on the right side. He holds the bottom of the baton in his left hand, and reaches all the way to the top of the stick with his other hand to tie the knot. The bundle is checked in red, orange, and yellow. The clown wears a red and yellow coat with a blue scarf, a green hat, and white gloves. He has a round red nose and light blue stubble on his chin, and he is smiling. Around the clown's hand at the bottom of the baton, there is a wavy blue circle. All around the card in the white space there are wavy confetti-like shapes in red, yellow, and light blue.

name: Two of Batons
interpretation: Back in the day, hobos came up with a series of symbols to pass on tips and help other hobos out. This clown is carving some of those symbols on a fence-post to mark the house in the distance. The upper symbol means 'a good road to follow,' and the lower two-columned symbol that our tramp clown is carving means 'the sky's the limit!'
image_description: A tramp clown kneels down on one knee, carving symbols into a fence-post with a knife. The symbol at the top of the fence post is a circle with a diagonal line through it. Below it, the clown is carving two parallel vertical lines. The clown has two yellow rubber chickens hanging from her belt. A purple tote bag decorated with gold flowers is at her side. A blue stick with a red and yellow striped bindle leans against the fence post. The clown's back is to the viewer. She wears a round green hat, a white vest with red polka dots, green houndstooth pants, a light blue shirt, white gloves, and scuffed red and yellow oversized shoes. She has a yellow wig and a red nose. The ground is sandy with small tufts of grass. A house and barn are visible in the distance, past the wire fence.

name: Three of Batons
interpretation: If you're a cynic, this tramp clown and his dog have been playing the sympathy card with their crutches and cane, but here they're dropping the act for a moment of spontaneous joy. If you're an optimist, then it's a miracle!
image_description: A grinning clown on crutches dances with a dog holding a cane. The clown dances on one leg, one arm on a crutch and the other arm waving in the air. He wears a spiky blue wig, a round red nose, a big ragged red trench cost, a purple and yellow polka dot scarf, green plaid pants, and purple and yellow oxford shoes. The dog is spotted and yellow with tufty fur and pointy ears. He stands on his hind legs, holding the cane in one paw, and he is about the same height as the clown. He has his eyes closed and his tongue hanging out of his mouth. The two crutches and the cane are red and blue with yellow accents. The ground is pale blue.

name: Four of Batons
interpretation: Fours often relate to routine and stability. There isn't much constancy in a hobo clown's life. But even when you're out tramping, you can't forgo the ritual of a good Sunday brunch. This clown has rolled up to Temperance's diner to put his feet up and plan his next move.
image_description: A tramp clown eating brunch in a booth in a diner, reading a newspaper. The clown is dressed in ragged brown and yellow clothes, including a big trench coat and plaid pants. The shoe on his visible foot is untied and busted: his toes poke through the front. His hat hangs from a hook on the bench, and there is a hole in its crown. He is bald on top with spiky red hair. He has a round red nose and light blue stubble on the bottom half of his face. He spreads himself across the bench of the booth. He holds a steaming cup of coffee in his left hand and a newspaper in his right hand. The bottom of the newspaper drags through a puddle of red ketchup that spills out of an overturned bottle on the table. He sips a chocolate milkshake through a comically long straw, made of four interlocked red and green bendy straws. In front of him, there is a partially eaten hamburger and French fries. There is another plate with fried eggs, sausage, and bacon in a smiley face. The diner booths are green and plush, and the floor is black and white checkered tiles.

name: Five of Batons
interpretation: Legend has it that Robert Johnson sold his soul to the Devil at a Mississippi crossroads in exchange for his guitar skills. It sounds sinister, but both Devil and Clown are happy with the deal (at the moment). This card is about experimenting, breaking new creative ground, and taking artistic risks.
image_description: A pleased clown and a devil walk in opposite directions away from a crossroads. The clown walks toward the viewer. The clown is strumming a guitar and whistling, indicated by colorful music notes. His eyes are closed. He wears pale blue overalls and a hat with a visor, a patched yellow shirt and red shoes. He has a blue wig and a round red nose. The devil walks away from the viewer, looking over his shoulder. He is all red, with short horns, a pointy nose, a spade-shaped tail, and furry legs. He holds his hands out in front of him, cupping a light blue balloon with an alarmed face painted on it. The road is sandy and the ground in the four quadrants around it is yellow and green. There is a signpost at the crossroads with two light blue signs painted with red double-headed arrows.

name: Six of Batons
interpretation: The character in this card comes from a truly rich history of shady traveling salesman, from Dolly Parton's mother-daughter two-timing "Traveling Man" to Flannery O'Connor's prosthetic-fetishist Bible salesman ("Good Country People"). Some days you're one of the young lovers running away to forge a new life, some days you're the mother protecting her home from the swindler, and some days you're just the little sister laughing at the ruckus.
image_description: Two young clown lovers getting kicked out of the house by a granny clown. Granny is wagging her finger and hitting the male clown on the head with a broom. She wears a sack-like brown dress with a purple and green ruffled collar and night-cap, and small half-moon glasses. The male clown is going down the steps, and the whack of the broom makes him close his eye, flail his arms, and drop his suitcase. He is wearing a green suit covered in patches and a puffy red wig. The female clown looks back in surprise. She carries another suitcase, and is wearing a floral orange and green dress and a yellow wig with roller curls. Each of the two suitcases that the lovers carry has a design of three crossed red, yellow, and blue batons on it. In the foreground, a pointy-hatted gnome crouches in the garden. In the background, a little girl clown with pigtails looks through the window and sticks out her tongue.

name: Seven of Batons
interpretation: In the spirit of the Star, three tramp clowns have come together to set up their own private paradise, a secluded campsite with enough roasted squirrel to go around.
image_description: Three tramp clowns sit around a campfire, where one clown is roasting a squirrel on a spit. The clown with the squirrel, on the left side of the card, is licking his chops. The clown in the middle leans back on a pack with his hat tipped over his eyes, sleeping with bubbles rising from his mouth. The clown on the right side of the card plays a harmonic. Behind the clowns, there is a red and white checkered tent. There is a river in the foreground and tall pine trees in the background. The seven batons are in the picture as follows. Three red, yellow, and blue logs support the fire. The squirrel is roasting on two batons. Another baton holds up the tent, and the last baton is stuck upright in the ground behind the harmonica player.

name: Eight of Batons
interpretation: The Eight of Batons is a tribute to one of the greatest pieces of music, the anthem of all wanderers under the moonlight – Patsy Cline's 'Walkin' After Midnight.' This card suggests it's time to take a break from the harsh day, and instead step out under cool lunar light and trust your intuition.
image_description: A clown wanders down the middle of a black road among green hills. She wears a red cow-girl outfit with yellow fringe and yellow baubles on the toes of her boots. She carries a red, yellow, and blue baton with a bindle hanging from it over her right shoulder. Seven more red, yellow, and blue batons form fence-posts on the sides of the road. The clown smiles slightly as she looks toward a weeping willow at the side of the road. The willow has a crying face on its trunk, with a red nose and three blue tears. A waning crescent moon is in the sky above, and reflected in a pool in the foreground.

name: Nine of Batons
interpretation: Nines typically signal a decline in energy, as the suit gets ready to wrap up. This tramp clown, rejected by polite society at every turn, is beginning to get downtrodden.
image_description: A tramp clown walks along a city street, looking discouraged with his head and arms hanging low. He is surrounded by barred windows. One of the windows is blocked with nine crossed red, blue, and yellow batons. Another window has a sign with an image of a crossed-out clown face. Behind him in a dark doorway, a person with an unhappy white face turns a hanging sign to an image of a red X. The clown wears a crushed brown hat and a ragged purple wig, with blue stubble painted on his face. He has a big, scuffed-up yellow and green trench coat, a long blue scarf, and brown plaid pants. His shoes are red with thick blue soles and the laces are missing.

name: Ten of Batons
interpretation: The solution to the tramp clown's woes is to leave the rails behind and take to the sky! This card moves from Fire to Air, and the suit of Swords.
image_description: A smiling and waving tramp clown rises through clouds in a hot air balloon. The balloon is red, green, and yellow, with a big smiling clown face on it. The basket is made of ten crossed red, yellow, and blue slats. The clown has a yellow wig and blue painted stubble on his chin, and wears a ragged brown and green trench coat with a purple scarf waving in the wind. There is a castle in the background among the puffy white clouds. In each of the upper corners, a clown face blows swirls of wind toward the balloon.

name: Page of Batons
interpretation: The Page of Batons is a young tramp clown hanging around down at the depot, waiting to hop his first train. He's a bit of an idealist – hopeful and excited about the wandering life.
image_description: A tramp clown leans against a railroad sign-post with a newspaper under his arm and a small dog at his side. The clown has his back to the viewer and his slightly smiling face is seen in profile. He rests his white-gloved left hand against the sign-post. He has wavy green hair, a white face, a red nose and a red cheek. His right hand is in his pocket, and there is a folded newspaper under his right arm. He wears a crushed red hat with a yellow flower in the brim, a long orange coat with tails, red and black checkered pants, and oversized green and yellow Oxford shoes. His clothing is covered in various patches. On the right side of the card, there is a small yellow dog with floppy ears. He looks up at the clown, raising an ear and a paw. The railroad sign is a yellow circle with a red X and the letters RR on it. Small lines suggest that it is lighting up. There is orange grass at the base of the post, and the ground is light blue with dark blue circles representing rocks.

name: Knight of Batons
interpretation: The Knight is based on the most famous of tramp clowns, Weary Willie. He is ready to blow this town, taking his little dog with him.
image_description: A tramp clown holding a stick and bindle climbs into a freight train car as a little dog hops up to join him. The clown has a neutral, if slightly surprised, expression. His face is painted white with blue stubble on the lower half. He has a round red nose and wavy yellow hair under a crushed brown porkpie hat. He wears a large brown coat with yellow lapels, a blue scarf, green vest, purple pants with yellow cuffs, and pointy-toed red shoes. His clothing is covered in scuffs and dirt. He sits on the edge of a red train car, lifting his right leg up to enter the darkness inside the car. In his left hand, he holds up a green stick with red shoots and a yellow bundle tied on its end. A small, short-haired white dog with pointy ears jumps up on its hind legs toward the train car. The clown looks down at the dog. The train car has an eight-spoked yellow wheel with a heart at the center. The ground is orange with yellow spots.

name: Queen of Batons
interpretation: The Queen of Batons has set up camp on a park bench. If you sit down by her, she'll play you some harmonica tunes and tell you about all the places she's been.
image_description: A smiling tramp clown holding a harmonica and a stick and bindle sits on a bench next to a small dog. The clown has bushy orange hair, a white face, and a round red nose. Her smile is painted red and she has black eyelashes painted below her eyes. She wears a blue and green newsboy cap with a green flower stuck to it. Her light blue shirt has a frilly collar that closes with an orange button. She also wears a red vest with a houndstooth pattern and four buttons shaped like yellow flowers. Her outfit is completed by puffy yellow plaid pants, round red shoes, and white gloves. She holds a purple and yellow harmonica in her lap with her left hand. In her right hand, she holds a green and yellow sticks with a polka-dotted bundle tied on the end. A brown down with pointy ears and a mustache sits next to her on the bench, looking straight ahead. The bench is red and yellow with a decorative heart shape motif in its arms. It is on the edge of a white path with brown rocks bordered by green grass.

name: King of Batons
interpretation: The King of Batons is panhandling with his old hound dog. He's ready to either berate passersby or look forlorn, as the situation calls for.
image_description: A worried-looking tramp clown stands with his hat out to collect money, accompanied by a dog. The clown has a white face with black stubble and a red-painted nose. He is bald on top and his red hair sticks out on the sides of his head. His eyebrows are raised. He wears a blue shirt and brown tweed pants with a green patch on the knee, held up with rope for a belt. Over this, he has a long yellow and orange checked coat with green lapels and a red flower on the breast. One of his red and white Oxford shoes is untied, the other has no laces at all. He stands leaning on a long green staff in his right hand, with his left foot up on a blue crate and his left hand on his hip. On the ground in front of the crate, there is a purple bowler hat with some money in it. A large white hound dog with blue spots sits next to the crate, looking at the viewer.
